seo.title: 100 Django resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Django resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource guide focuses on modernizing Django workflows for 2024, specifically addressing the shift toward asynchronous operations, AI/ML model integration, and the 'HTML-over-the-wire' frontend approach. It provides actionable tools and patterns to scale Django applications beyond traditional monolithic structures.

items.title: django-htmx integration
items.description: Middleware and helper functions to handle HTMX headers in Django views, allowing for partial template rendering and reduced JavaScript complexity.

items.title: Django Template Partials
items.description: Utilize 'django-template-partials' to define reusable segments within a single HTML file, facilitating HTMX fragment updates without splitting files.

items.title: Alpine.js for Client-Side State
items.description: Implement Alpine.js alongside Django templates to handle UI states like modals and dropdowns without a full SPA framework.

items.title: django-tailwind
items.description: A package to integrate Tailwind CSS into Django projects with a built-in management command for the JIT compiler.

items.title: Django-Vite Integration
items.description: Use 'django-vite' to bridge the Django backend with a Vite-powered frontend for modern asset bundling and Hot Module Replacement.

items.title: Django-Components
items.description: Provides a way to create encapsulated template components with their own logic, similar to React components but server-side.

items.title: Cotton for Django Templates
items.description: A modern component system for Django templates that brings Vue/React-like syntax to standard .html files.

items.title: CSRF for Fetch/Axios
items.description: Implementation of the 'X-CSRFToken' header in JavaScript requests to ensure secure communication with Django's middleware.

items.title: Django-Browser-Reload
items.description: Development tool that automatically reloads the browser when templates or static files are modified.

items.title: Compressor and ManifestStaticFilesStorage
items.description: Configure Django's built-in storage and django-compressor for efficient asset versioning and minification in production.

items.title: Async Views and sync_to_async
items.description: Proper usage of 'asgiref.sync.sync_to_async' to wrap ORM calls within async view functions to prevent event loop blocking.

items.title: StreamingHttpResponse for LLMs
items.description: Using Django's StreamingHttpResponse to stream tokens from OpenAI or local LLM providers directly to the client.

items.title: Celery with Redis Broker
items.description: Standard setup for offloading heavy ML inference or data processing tasks to background workers.

items.title: Django-Lifecycle-Hooks
items.description: Alternative to signals for triggering ML model updates or cache invalidation directly within model methods.

items.title: Pgvector Integration
items.description: Using 'django-pgvector' to store and query vector embeddings directly within PostgreSQL from Django models.

items.title: Daphne or Uvicorn for ASGI
items.description: Configuring ASGI servers to support WebSockets and async features that Gunicorn cannot handle alone.

items.title: Django-Q2
items.description: A native Django task queue that uses the ORM or Redis, providing a simpler alternative to Celery for smaller clusters.

items.title: ONNX Runtime Integration
items.description: Serving exported ML models using ONNX Runtime within a Django view for low-latency inference without heavy frameworks.

items.title: Scrapy-Django-Item
items.description: Pipeline for saving scraped data directly into Django models, useful for building data-driven AI applications.

items.title: Django-Watcher for File Tasks
items.description: Monitoring local directories for new data files to trigger automated Django management commands or tasks.

items.title: DRF Select Related and Prefetch Related
items.description: Eliminating N+1 query problems in Django REST Framework by optimizing queryset execution in the get_queryset method.

items.title: Django-Debug-Toolbar
items.description: Essential development tool for inspecting SQL queries, cache hits, and template rendering performance.

items.title: Django-Silk Profiling
items.description: Live profiling tool for identifying bottlenecks in complex request-response cycles and database interactions.

items.title: Database Indexing Strategies
items.description: Using 'indexes' in Model Meta classes to create B-Tree or GIN indexes for frequently filtered fields.

items.title: Django-Health-Check
items.description: Adding monitoring endpoints for database, disk, and cache health, compatible with Kubernetes liveness probes.

items.title: Redis for Session and Cache Storage
items.description: Configuring 'django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache' to replace default database-backed sessions for high-traffic sites.

items.title: Pytest-Django for Parallel Testing
items.description: Using 'pytest -n auto' with pytest-django to run test suites across multiple CPU cores to reduce CI time.

items.title: Django-CORS-Headers
items.description: Configuring Cross-Origin Resource Sharing for decoupled architectures where the frontend resides on a different domain.

items.title: PostgreSQL Partitioning with django-postgres-extra
items.description: Managing extremely large datasets by implementing table partitioning directly through Django models.

items.title: Gunicorn Sync vs Threaded Workers
items.description: Optimizing worker class selection based on the I/O profile of the application to maximize throughput.

pro_tips: Use 'select_related' for ForeignKey/OneToOne and 'prefetch_related' for ManyToMany to solve 90% of performance issues.
Avoid putting business logic in Serializers or Views; move it to Service layers or Model methods for better testability.
When using HTMX, return only the necessary HTML fragment using 'request.htmx' checks to save bandwidth and server CPU.
Always use a custom User model at the start of every project, even if the default seems sufficient, to avoid painful migrations later.
For ML projects, perform model loading in the AppConfig.ready() method to ensure models are loaded once into memory at startup.
